data.narrative:
AstroForge is a Series A space & aerospace company that has raised $53M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 4 private peers at the same or adjacent stage (ClearSpace, Cowboy Space Corporation, Aalyria, and Hubble Network), with valuations ranging from $400M to $2.0B and a median of $850M. A broader comp set includes 5 public space & aerospace equities (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) trading at a median 64.2x price-to-sales, though this method cannot be applied without AstroForge's revenue figure.

Two applicable triangulation methods support the valuation. Stage-progression analysis, using the $53M raised as a proxy for post-money value and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage step-ups, implies a valuation of $106M. Peer-median analysis across the 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields $850M. The disconnect between these methods reflects stage and maturity differences within the peer set, particularly the inclusion of Series B-stage companies.

The triangulated range spans $90M (low) to $977M (high), with a median of $478M. Key uncertainties include revenue scale and near-term capital intensity, both of which would refine positioning within this band. The wide range reflects genuine heterogeneity in the early-stage space sector rather than valuation precision.
